content_hints.description: The trench ends and the blasted fields begin. Shell craters pock the grey earth like open sores, their edges crusted with frost. Abandoned equipment lies half-buried in the mud -- a broken cart wheel, a legionnaire's pack with the straps cut, a standard pole snapped in two. The silence is worse than shelling.
content_hints.read_aloud: You climb over the last sandbag wall and into no man's land. The ground is cratered and grey. Nothing moves except the ash drifting down from the burning city.
event_triggers: Boot prints in the mud -- some heading west, many more heading east. The retreating kind.

content_hints.description: A massive crater serves as a natural crossroads. At its center, a toppled siege engine lies on its side, wheels still turning in the wind. The crater rim offers a clear sightline in every direction -- which means anything out there can see you, too. Dark shapes shuffle along the far ridge.
content_hints.read_aloud: A massive crater with a toppled siege engine at its center, wheels still turning lazily in the wind. The rim gives you a clear view in every direction -- which means anything out there has a clear view of you. On the far ridge, dark shapes move against the orange glow of the burning city.
event_triggers: The horizon glows orange where the Aldermani city burns. The light pulses, as if the fire has a heartbeat.
content_hints.perception_success: Tracks in the crater mud suggest the Hounds circle this position regularly -- a patrol route. They'll return within the hour.

content_hints.description: The field narrows between two collapsed farmsteads. Stone walls provide cover but also funnel movement into a killing ground. A Legionnaire's body lies propped against the eastern wall, arms folded across his chest -- someone took the time to close his eyes. His ammunition pouch is empty.
content_hints.read_aloud: Collapsed farmsteads narrow the field into a funnel of broken stone walls. A Legionnaire sits propped against the eastern wall, arms folded, eyes closed -- someone gave him that dignity. His ammunition pouch is empty. Beyond the walls, something howls in the distance. Not a wolf.
content_hints.investigation_success: The dead Legionnaire's journal is tucked inside his coat. The last entry reads: 'They don't stop. They never stop. But the Knight -- the Knight watches. It's learning.'
event_triggers: Something howls in the distance. Not a wolf. Wolves don't howl like they're in pain.
